edges.description: Impaired renal anion exchanger function disrupts distal acid secretion.
Altered erythrocyte anion exchanger function destabilizes red-cell membranes.
Acidification failure produces the clinical distal renal tubular acidosis phenotype.
Failed distal urinary acidification causes systemic metabolic acidosis.
Distal RTA can produce hypokalemia as part of the electrolyte phenotype.
intermediate_mechanisms: renal potassium wasting in distal renal tubular acidosis

edges.description: Distal RTA produces a hyperchloremic metabolic acidosis pattern.
Distal RTA predisposes to nephrocalcinosis in SLC4A1-related disease.
intermediate_mechanisms: persistently alkaline urine and calcium salt precipitation

edges.description: Pediatric distal RTA can impair growth.
intermediate_mechanisms: chronic metabolic acidosis and mineral bone disease

edges.description: Pediatric distal RTA can cause rickets through chronic acid-base disturbance.
intermediate_mechanisms: chronic metabolic acidosis and skeletal mineralization impairment

edges.description: Decreased serum bicarbonate reports systemic metabolic acidosis.
Inappropriately alkaline urine is a direct readout of failed distal urinary acidification.
Low serum potassium reports the electrolyte branch of distal RTA.
intermediate_mechanisms: renal potassium wasting in distal renal tubular acidosis

edges.description: Hyperchloremia accompanies the metabolic acidosis pattern of distal RTA.
Red-cell membrane instability causes hemolytic anemia.
Red-cell membrane instability produces spherocytic red-cell morphology.
Red-cell membrane instability can produce elliptocytic red-cell morphology.
Chronic hemolysis increases bilirubin and can produce jaundice.
Chronic hemolysis can produce hepatosplenomegaly in severe cases.
intermediate_mechanisms: chronic hemolysis and reticuloendothelial red-cell clearance

edges.description: Lower hemoglobin is the measured blood readout of hemolytic anemia severity.
Increased bilirubin reports heme breakdown during hemolysis.
Low serum bicarbonate reports the metabolic acidosis caused by failed distal acid excretion.
High urine pH during metabolic acidosis reports failure of distal urinary acidification.
Low serum potassium reports the electrolyte disturbance downstream of distal RTA.
High chloride reports the hyperchloremic metabolic acidosis branch of distal RTA.
Lower hemoglobin tracks anemia severity from erythrocyte membrane instability.
Increased bilirubin reports hemolysis-driven heme breakdown.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: Total bilirubin was 4.9 mg/dL with indirect bilirubin 4.7 mg/dL, and serum alkaline phosphatase was elevated at 530 IU/L.

readouts.interpretation: Increased bilirubin reports hemolysis-driven heme breakdown.

nodes.description: Red blood cell transfusion may be required for marked or transfusion-dependent hemolytic anemia in severe reported cases.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: He was transfused and treated with oral sodium bicarbonate and potassium citrate.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: However, patient 2 is severely anemic with dependence on frequent transfusion, and is at risk for progressive nephrotoxicity from renal hemosiderosis (8) and chronic scarring (9) in the setting of nephrocalcinosis.

nodes.description: Distal nephron acidification failure prevents appropriate urinary acidification, causing metabolic acidosis and electrolyte complications.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: Additional findings of “metabolic acidosis with alkaline urine pH” (detailed medical records are unavailable) led to the clinical diagnosis of distal renal tubular acidosis.

evidence.reference_title: Rare Case of Hemolytic Anemia and Distal Renal Tubular Acidosis in an adult due to Homozygous SLC4A1 Mutation.
evidence.snippet: Distal renal tubular acidosis (dRTA) can be caused by mutations in SLC4A1, which encodes the Cl-/HCO3- exchanger of the renal type A intercalated cell, kidney AE1.

nodes.description: Impaired distal urinary acidification with systemic metabolic acidosis.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: We report here two unrelated Indian patients with combined hemolytic anemia and dRTA who share homozygous A858D mutations of the AE1/SLC4A1 gene.

nodes.description: Elliptocytic red-cell morphology is reported in homozygous A858D cases.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: Peripheral blood smear was remarkable for spherostomatocytosis, anisopoikilocytosis, acanthocytosis, and elliptocytes.

nodes.description: Pediatric cases can present with impaired growth.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: Patient 1, a 15 year old from Maharasthtra of Kunbi caste born of a non-consanguinous marriage, presented at age 6 years with growth retardation, jaundice and a history of fracture on falling.

evidence.reference_title: Rare Case of Hemolytic Anemia and Distal Renal Tubular Acidosis in an adult due to Homozygous SLC4A1 Mutation.
evidence.snippet: In this case study, we report an adult patient presenting with generalized weakness, marked anemia, spherocytosis, and no features of thalassemia.

readouts.interpretation: Lower hemoglobin tracks anemia severity from erythrocyte membrane instability.

nodes.description: Chronic hemolysis due to red-cell membrane dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Rare Case of Hemolytic Anemia and Distal Renal Tubular Acidosis in an adult due to Homozygous SLC4A1 Mutation.
evidence.snippet: In some rare instances, SLC4A1 dRTA can present with hemolytic anemia resulting in marked anemia that is not responsive to standard interventions.

nodes.description: Combined liver and spleen enlargement was documented in the severe pediatric case.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: Physical examination revealed hepatosplenomegaly.

nodes.description: Hyperchloremia accompanies the metabolic acidosis pattern of distal RTA.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations and clinical characteristics of dRTA caused by SLC4A1 mutations: Analysis based on published patients.
evidence.snippet: The children present with metabolic acidosis with high urinary pH, accompanying hypokalemia, hyperchloremia, nephrocalcinosis, growth retardation and hematological abnormalities should be suspected as dRTA and suggested a genetic testing.

nodes.description: Low serum potassium can accompany the distal renal tubular acidosis phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Metabolic acidosis was noted concomitant with spot urine pH of 8 and absence of glucosuria.

nodes.description: Oral sodium bicarbonate and potassium citrate correct systemic acidosis in reported patients, although nephrocalcinosis may persist and hematologic severity can vary.

evidence.snippet: He was transfused and treated with oral sodium bicarbonate and potassium citrate.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: Both patients responded to oral base supplementation with resolution of acidosis, but without reversal of nephrocalcinosis.

nodes.description: Abnormal erythrocyte membrane anion exchange causes red-cell fragility and chronic hemolysis.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: The presence of band 3-deficient ovalocytic hemolytic anemia together with evidence of distal renal tubular acidosis suggested possible mutation of the SLC4A1 gene encoding erythroid and kidney isoforms of Band 3/AE1/SLC4A1.

evidence.reference_title: Hematological abnormalities in patients with distal renal tubular acidosis and hemoglobinopathies.
evidence.snippet: Mutations of the human SLC4A1 gene encoding erythroid and kidney isoforms of anion exchanger 1 (AE1, band 3) result in erythrocyte abnormalities or distal renal tubular acidosis (dRTA) and such mutations are observed in Southeast Asia, where hemoglobinopathies are prevalent.

nodes.description: Skeletal mineralization defects can accompany pediatric distal renal tubular acidosis.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: Physical exam revealed a weight of 13 kg (<5th %ile for age) and rickets.

evidence.reference_title: Genotypic analysis of SLC4A1 A858D mutation in Indian population associated with distal renal tubular Acidosis (dRTA) coupled with hemolytic anemia.
evidence.snippet: We here described the clinical, hematological and genetic data of 16 individuals from 12 families having AR dRTA coupled with HS. All patients carried homozygous SLC4A1 (A858D) mutation, whereas their family members had heterozygous A858D obtained by HRM analysis and confirmed by RFLP and Sanger sequencing.

evidence.reference_title: Hemolytic anemia and distal renal tubular acidosis in two Indian patients homozygous for SLC4A1/AE1 mutation A858D.
evidence.snippet: As shown in Fig. 2C, nominally nonconsanguinous patient 1 was homozygous for the SLC4A1/AE1/Band 3 missense mutation A858D, encoded by a C-to-A transversion changing codon GCC to GAC in exon 19.

nodes.description: Biallelic pathogenic SLC4A1 variants impair anion exchanger 1 function in kidney collecting-duct alpha-intercalated cells and in erythrocytes.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations and clinical characteristics of dRTA caused by SLC4A1 mutations: Analysis based on published patients.
evidence.snippet: The age of onset was younger (P < 0.01), urine pH was higher (P < 0.01), and serum potassium was lower (P < 0.001) in recessive patients than patients with dominant SLC4A1 mutations.
